title: Marbury v Madison
text:
Title: Marbury v Madison
Citation: 5 U.S. (1 Cranch) 137 (1803)
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/5/137/

The Supreme Court held that it had authority to say what the law is and to decline to apply a statute inconsistent with the Constitution. Marbury is the canonical source for judicial review and the relationship between constitutional supremacy and ordinary legislation.

title: Brown v Board of Education
text:
Title: Brown v Board of Education
Citation: 347 U.S. 483 (1954)
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/347/483/

The Court held that racial segregation in public schools violates equal protection because separate educational facilities are inherently unequal. Brown is foundational for equal protection, desegregation, strict scrutiny development, and constitutional anti-subordination principles.

title: Youngstown Sheet and Tube Co. v Sawyer
source_name: Youngstown Sheet and Tube Co. v Sawyer
text:
Title: Youngstown Sheet and Tube Co. v Sawyer
Citation: 343 U.S. 579 (1952)
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/343/579/

The Court invalidated President Truman's seizure of steel mills during the Korean War. Justice Jackson's concurrence supplies the tripartite framework for presidential power: maximum authority with Congress, a zone of twilight without congressional action, and lowest ebb against Congress.

title: Chevron U.S.A. Inc. v Natural Resources Defense Council
source_name: Chevron U.S.A. Inc. v Natural Resources Defense Council
text:
Title: Chevron U.S.A. Inc. v Natural Resources Defense Council
Citation: 467 U.S. 837 (1984)
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/467/837/

Chevron formerly required courts to defer to reasonable agency interpretations of ambiguous statutes. Researchers must account for the Supreme Court's later overruling of Chevron deference in Loper Bright Enterprises v Raimondo, while older cases may remain relevant historically and for prior administrative law doctrine.

title: Loper Bright Enterprises v Raimondo
text:
Title: Loper Bright Enterprises v Raimondo
Citation: 603 U.S. ___ (2024)
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://www.supremecourt.gov/opinions/23pdf/22-451_7m58.pdf

The Supreme Court overruled Chevron deference and held that courts must exercise independent judgment in deciding whether an agency acted within statutory authority. The case is now central to statutory interpretation, administrative law, and judicial review of agency interpretations.
